15: 
16:   if (!command) {
17:     console.log('用法: trae <command> [args]');

1: import { TraeExecutor, TraeTaskConfig } from '../utils/trae-executor';
2: import { ContextBridge } from '../utils/context-bridge';
3: 
4: const executor = new TraeExecutor();
5: const bridge = new ContextBridge();
6: 
7: export async function runTask(args: string[]) {
8:   const config: TraeTaskConfig = { prompt: '' };
9:   const promptParts: string[] = [];
10: 
11:   for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
12:     const arg = args[i];
13: 
14:     if (arg === '--background') {
15:       config.background = true;
16:     } else if (arg === '--json') {
17:       config.jsonOutput = true;
18:     } else if (arg === '--yolo' || arg === '-y') {
19:       config.yolo = true;
20:     } else if (arg === '--resume') {
21:       if (args[i + 1] && !args[i + 1].startsWith('-')) {
22:         config.resume = args[i + 1];
23:         i++;
24:       } else {
25:         config.resume = 'AUTO';
26:       }
27:     } else if (arg.startsWith('--resume=')) {
28:       config.resume = arg.substring('--resume='.length);
29:     } else if (arg === '--session-id' && args[i + 1]) {
30:       config.sessionId = args[i + 1];
31:       i++;
32:     } else if ((arg === '--worktree' || arg === '-w') && args[i + 1] && !args[i + 1].startsWith('-')) {
33:       config.worktree = args[i + 1];
34:       i++;
35:     } else if (arg === '--worktree' || arg === '-w') {
36:       config.worktree = '__auto__';
37:     } else if (arg.startsWith('--worktree=')) {
38:       config.worktree = arg.substring('--worktree='.length);
39:     } else if (arg === '--allowed-tool' && args[i + 1]) {
40:       config.allowedTools = config.allowedTools || [];
41:       config.allowedTools.push(args[i + 1]);
42:       i++;
43:     } else if (arg === '--disallowed-tool' && args[i + 1]) {
44:       config.disallowedTools = config.disallowedTools || [];
45:       config.disallowedTools.push(args[i + 1]);
46:       i++;
47:     } else if (arg === '--query-timeout' && args[i + 1]) {
48:       config.queryTimeout = args[i + 1];
49:       i++;
50:     } else if (arg === '--bash-tool-timeout' && args[i + 1]) {
51:       config.bashToolTimeout = args[i + 1];
52:       i++;
53:     } else if (arg === '-c' && args[i + 1]) {

56:       const eqIdx = override.indexOf('=');
57:       if (eqIdx > 0) {
58:         config.configOverrides[override.substring(0, eqIdx)] = override.substring(eqIdx + 1);
59:       }
60:       i++;
61:     } else if (arg === '--inject-context' && args[i + 1]) {

92:     console.log(`使用隔离 worktree: ${config.worktree === '__auto__' ? '自动生成' : config.worktree}`);
93:   }

1: import { AcpServerManager } from '../utils/acp-server-manager';
2: 
3: const serverManager = new AcpServerManager();
4: 
5: export async function acp(args: string[]) {
6:   const action = args[0] || 'status';
7: 
8:   switch (action) {
9:     case 'start':
10:       return startServer(args);
11:     case 'stop':
12:       return stopServer();
13:     case 'status':
14:       return serverStatus();
15:     case 'agents':
16:       return listAgents();
17:     case 'run':
18:       return runViaAcp(args);
19:     case 'stream':
20:       return streamViaAcp(args);
21:     default:
22:       console.log('用法: /trae:acp <action> [options]');
23:       console.log('动作:');
24:       console.log('  start    启动 ACP Server');
25:       console.log('  stop     停止 ACP Server');
26:       console.log('  status   查看服务器状态');
27:       console.log('  agents   发现可用 Agent');
28:       console.log('  run      通过 ACP 执行任务');
29:       console.log('  stream   通过 ACP 流式执行任务');
30:   }
31: }
32: 
33: async function startServer(args: string[]) {
34:   const options: { yolo?: boolean; allowedTools?: string[]; disabledTools?: string[] } = {};
35: 
36:   for (let i = 1; i < args.length; i++) {
37:     if (args[i] === '--yolo') {
38:       options.yolo = true;
39:     }
40:     if (args[i] === '--allowed-tool' && args[i + 1]) {
41:       options.allowedTools = options.allowedTools || [];
42:       options.allowedTools.push(args[i + 1]);
43:       i++;
44:     }
45:     if (args[i] === '--disabled-tool' && args[i + 1]) {
46:       options.disabledTools = options.disabledTools || [];
47:       options.disabledTools.push(args[i + 1]);
48:       i++;
49:     }
50:   }
51: 
52:   console.log('正在启动 ACP Server...');
53: 
54:   try {
55:     await serverManager.start(options);
56:     console.log('\n✅ ACP Server 已启动');
57:     console.log('  传输: STDIO JSON-RPC');
58:     console.log(`\n使用 /trae:acp run "任务" 执行任务`);
59:     console.log(`使用 /trae:acp stream "任务" 流式执行`);
60:     // Keep the process alive so the server stays running
61:     await new Promise(() => {});
62:   } catch (error: any) {
63:     console.error(`❌ 启动失败: ${error.message}`);
64:   }
65: }
66: 
67: async function stopServer() {
68:   if (!serverManager.isRunning()) {
69:     console.log('ACP Server 未运行。');
70:     return;
71:   }
72: 
73:   console.log('正在停止 ACP Server...');
74:   await serverManager.stop();
75:   console.log('✅ ACP Server 已停止。');
76: }
77: 
78: async function serverStatus() {
79:   const status = serverManager.getStatus();
80: 
81:   console.log('\n## ACP Server 状态\n');

1: import { spawn } from 'child_process';
2: import path from 'path';
3: 
4: export async function handleHook(args: string[]) {
5:     const hookType = args[0];
6: 
7:     if (!hookType) {
8:         console.error('Usage: trae-plugin-cc hooks <session-start|session-end|stop-gate>');
9:         process.exit(1);
10:     }
11: 
12:     const hookMap: Record<string, { script: string; arg: string }> = {
13:         'session-start': { script: 'session-lifecycle-hook.mjs', arg: 'SessionStart' },
14:         'session-end': { script: 'session-lifecycle-hook.mjs', arg: 'SessionEnd' },
15:         'stop-gate': { script: 'stop-review-gate-hook.mjs', arg: '' },
16:     };
17: 
18:     const entry = hookMap[hookType];
19:     if (!entry) {
20:         console.error(`Unknown hook type: ${hookType}`);
21:         console.error(`Available: ${Object.keys(hookMap).join(', ')}`);
22:         process.exit(1);
23:     }
24: 
25:     const pluginRoot = process.env.CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT || path.join(__dirname, '..');
26:     const scriptPath = path.join(pluginRoot, 'scripts', entry.script);
27: 
28:     const spawnArgs = entry.arg ? [scriptPath, entry.arg] : [scriptPath];
29: 
30:     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
31:         const child = spawn('node', spawnArgs, {
32:             stdio: 'inherit',
33:             detached: false
34:         });
35: 
36:         child.on('close', (code) => {
37:             if (code === 0) {
38:                 resolve(undefined);
39:             } else {
40:                 reject(new Error(`Hook exited with code ${code}`));
41:             }
42:         });
43: 
44:         child.on('error', reject);
45:     });
46: }

1: import { spawn, ChildProcess } from 'child_process';
2: import * as fs from 'fs';
3: import * as path from 'path';
4: import { AuthBridge } from './auth-bridge';
5: import { getPluginDir } from '../config';
6: 
7: export interface TraeTaskConfig {
8:   prompt: string;
9:   background?: boolean;
10:   jsonOutput?: boolean;
11:   yolo?: boolean;
12:   allowedTools?: string[];
13:   disallowedTools?: string[];
14:   sessionId?: string;
15:   resume?: string;
16:   worktree?: string;
17:   queryTimeout?: string;
18:   bashToolTimeout?: string;
19:   configOverrides?: Record<string, string>;
20: }
21: 
22: export interface TraeTaskResult {
23:   taskId: string;
24:   output: string;
25:   exitCode: number | null;
26:   sessionId?: string;
27:   duration: number;
28:   jsonOutput?: any;
29: }
30: 
31: const PLUGIN_DIR = getPluginDir();
32: 
33: function ensurePluginDir() {
34:   if (!fs.existsSync(PLUGIN_DIR)) {
35:     fs.mkdirSync(PLUGIN_DIR, { recursive: true });
36:   }
37: }
38: 
39: export class TraeExecutor {
40:   private authBridge: AuthBridge;
41: 
42:   constructor() {
43:     this.authBridge = new AuthBridge();
44:   }
45: 
46:   async execute(config: TraeTaskConfig): Promise<TraeTaskResult> {
47:     ensurePluginDir();
48: 
49:     const taskId = Date.now().toString();
50:     const logFile = path.join(PLUGIN_DIR, `${taskId}.log`);
51:     const pidFile = path.join(PLUGIN_DIR, `${taskId}.pid`);
52:     const args = this.buildArgs(config);
53:     const env = this.authBridge.buildSpawnEnv();
54:     const startTime = Date.now();
55: 
56:     if (config.background) {
57:       return this.executeBackground(args, env, taskId, logFile, pidFile, startTime);
58:     }
59: 
60:     return this.executeForeground(args, env, taskId, logFile, pidFile, startTime, config.jsonOutput);
61:   }
62: 
63:   private buildArgs(config: TraeTaskConfig): string[] {
64:     const args: string[] = [];
65: 
66:     if (config.allowedTools) {
67:       for (const tool of config.allowedTools) {
68:         args.push('--allowed-tool', tool);
69:       }
70:     }
71: 
72:     if (config.disallowedTools) {
73:       for (const tool of config.disallowedTools) {
74:         args.push('--disallowed-tool', tool);
75:       }
76:     }
77: 
78:     if (config.yolo) args.push('--yolo');
79:     if (config.queryTimeout) args.push('--query-timeout', config.queryTimeout);
80:     if (config.bashToolTimeout) args.push('--bash-tool-timeout', config.bashToolTimeout);
81:     if (config.sessionId) args.push('--session-id', config.sessionId);
82:     if (config.resume) {
83:       if (config.resume === 'AUTO') {
84:         args.push('--resume');
85:       } else {
86:         args.push('--resume', config.resume);
87:       }
88:     }
89:     if (config.worktree) {
90:       if (config.worktree === '__auto__') {
91:         args.push('--worktree');
92:       } else {
93:         args.push('--worktree', config.worktree);
94:       }
95:     }
96: 
97:     if (config.configOverrides) {
98:       for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(config.configOverrides)) {
99:         args.push('-c', `${k}=${v}`);
100:       }
101:     }
102: 
103:     args.push('--print');
104: 
105:     if (config.jsonOutput) {
106:       args.push('--json');
107:     }
108: 
109:     args.push(config.prompt);
110: 
111:     return args;
112:   }
113: 
114:   private executeBackground(
115:     args: string[], env: NodeJS.ProcessEnv, taskId: string,
116:     logFile: string, pidFile: string, startTime: number
117:   ): TraeTaskResult {
118:     const out = fs.openSync(logFile, 'a');
119:     const err = fs.openSync(logFile, 'a');
120: 
121:     const child = spawn('trae-cli', args, {
122:       detached: true,
123:       stdio: ['ignore', out, err],
124:       env,
125:     });
126: 
127:     child.unref();
128: 
129:     if (child.pid) {
130:       fs.writeFileSync(pidFile, child.pid.toString());
131:     }
132: 
133:     return {
134:       taskId,
135:       output: `任务已在后台启动 (ID: ${taskId})\n日志文件: ${logFile}`,
136:       exitCode: null,
137:       duration: Date.now() - startTime,
138:     };
139:   }
140: 
141:   private executeForeground(
142:     args: string[], env: NodeJS.ProcessEnv, taskId: string,
143:     logFile: string, pidFile: string, startTime: number,
144:     parseJson: boolean = false
145:   ): Promise<TraeTaskResult> {
146:     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
147:       const child = spawn('trae-cli', args, {
148:         stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
149:         env,
150:       });
151: 
152:       if (child.pid) {
153:         fs.writeFileSync(pidFile, child.pid.toString());
154:       }
155: 
156:       let combinedOutput = '';
157:       let settled = false;
158: 
159:       const append = (chunk: Buffer) => {
160:         const text = chunk.toString();
161:         combinedOutput += text;
162:         fs.appendFileSync(logFile, text);
163:       };
164: 
165:       const settle = (result: TraeTaskResult) => {
166:         if (settled) return;
167:         settled = true;
168:         if (fs.existsSync(pidFile)) fs.unlinkSync(pidFile);
169:         resolve(result);
170:       };
171: 
172:       const fail = (error: Error) => {
173:         if (settled) return;
174:         settled = true;
175:         if (fs.existsSync(pidFile)) fs.unlinkSync(pidFile);
176:         child.kill('SIGKILL');
177:         reject(error);
178:       };
179: 
180:       const TIMEOUT_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000;
181:       const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
182:         fail(new Error(`任务执行超时 (300s)`));
183:       }, TIMEOUT_MS);
184: 
185:       child.stdout?.on('data', append);
186:       child.stderr?.on('data', append);
187: 
188:       child.on('error', (error) => {
189:         clearTimeout(timeout);
190:         fail(new Error(`执行失败: ${error.message}`));
191:       });
192: 
193:       child.on('close', (code) => {
194:         clearTimeout(timeout);
195: 
196:         let jsonOutput: any = undefined;
197:         let sessionId: string | undefined;
198: 
199:         if (parseJson && combinedOutput.trim()) {
200:           try {
201:             jsonOutput = JSON.parse(combinedOutput);
202:             sessionId = jsonOutput?.session_id;
203:           } catch {}
204:         }
205: 
206:         settle({
207:           taskId,
208:           output: combinedOutput,
209:           exitCode: code,
210:           sessionId,
211:           duration: Date.now() - startTime,
212:           jsonOutput,
213:         });
214:       });
215:     });
216:   }
217: }

(End of file - total 217 lines)
</content>

88: export class SessionReader {
89:   private sessionsDir: string;

91: 
92:   constructor() {
93:     const candidates = getCliCacheDirs();
94:     const cacheDir = candidates.find(dir => fs.existsSync(dir)) || candidates[0];
95: 
96:     this.sessionsDir = path.join(cacheDir, 'sessions');
97:     this.historyFile = path.join(cacheDir, 'history.jsonl');
98:   }
99: 
100:   listSessions(options?: { cwd?: string; limit?: number }): SessionMeta[] {
101:     if (!fs.existsSync(this.sessionsDir)) return [];
102: 
103:     const sessions = fs.readdirSync(this.sessionsDir)
104:       .filter(dir => {
105:         const sessionFile = path.join(this.sessionsDir, dir, 'session.json');
106:         return fs.existsSync(sessionFile);
107:       })
108:       .map(dir => {
109:         try {
110:           const content = fs.readFileSync(
111:             path.join(this.sessionsDir, dir, 'session.json'),
112:             'utf-8'
113:           );
114:           return JSON.parse(content) as SessionMeta;
115:         } catch {
116:           return null;
117:         }
118:       })
119:       .filter((s): s is SessionMeta => s !== null)
120:       .sort((a, b) =>
121:         new Date(b.updated_at).getTime() - new Date(a.updated_at).getTime()
122:       );
123: 
124:     let filtered = sessions;
125:     if (options?.cwd) {
126:       filtered = filtered.filter(s => s.metadata.cwd === options.cwd);
127:     }
128: 
129:     if (options?.limit) {
130:       filtered = filtered.slice(0, options.limit);
131:     }
132:

136:   getSession(sessionId: string): SessionMeta | null {
137:     const filePath = path.join(this.sessionsDir, sessionId, 'session.json');

141:     } catch {

147:     const filePath = path.join(this.sessionsDir, sessionId, 'events.jsonl');
148:     if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) return [];
149: 
150:     const lines = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8').split('\n');
151:     return lines
152:       .filter(line => line.trim())
153:       .map(line => {
154:         try { return JSON.parse(line) as SessionEvent; }
155:         catch { return null; }
156:       })
157:       .filter((e): e is SessionEvent => e !== null);
158:   }
159: 
160:   getConversation(sessionId: string, options?: { limit?: number }): ConversationMessage[] {
161:     const events = this.getEvents(sessionId);
162:     let messages = events
163:       .filter(e => e.message)
164:       .map(e => {
165:         const msg = e.message!.message;
166:         let content = '';
167:         if (typeof msg.content === 'string') {
168:           content = msg.content;
169:         } else if (Array.isArray(msg.content)) {
170:           content = msg.content.map(c => c.text || '').join('\n');
171:         }
172: 
173:         return {
174:           role: msg.role,
175:           content,
176:           toolCalls: msg.tool_calls?.map(tc => tc.function.name),
177:           timestamp: e.created_at,
178:         };
179:       });
180: 
181:     if (options?.limit) {
182:       messages = messages.slice(-options.limit);
183:     }
184: 
185:     return messages;
186:   }
187: 
188:   getToolCalls(sessionId: string): ToolCallRecord[] {
189:     const events = this.getEvents(sessionId);
190:     const callMap = new Map<string, ToolCallRecord>();
191: 
192:     for (const e of events) {
193:       if (e.tool_call) {
194:         callMap.set(e.tool_call.tool_call_id, {
195:           id: e.tool_call.tool_call_id,
196:           name: e.tool_call.tool_info.name,
197:           input: e.tool_call.input,
198:           timestamp: e.created_at,
199:         });
200:       }
201:     }
202: 
203:     for (const e of events) {
204:       if (e.tool_call_output) {
205:         const call = callMap.get(e.tool_call_output.tool_call_id);
206:         if (call) {
207:           call.output = e.tool_call_output.output;
208:           call.isError = e.tool_call_output.is_error;
209:         }
210:       }
211:     }
212: 
213:     return Array.from(callMap.values());
214:   }
215: 
216:   getFileTrackStatus(sessionId: string): FileTrackStatus {
217:     const events = this.getEvents(sessionId);
218:     for (const e of events) {

220:         return e.state_update.updates.file_track_status;
221:       }
222:     }
223:     return {};
224:   }

235:     );
236:     return match || null;

238:

242:

244:     const toolCalls = this.getToolCalls(sessionId);
245: 
246:     let summary = `## 会话: ${meta.metadata.title}\n`;
247:     summary += `- ID: ${meta.id}\n`;
248:     summary += `- 工作目录: ${meta.metadata.cwd}\n`;
249:     summary += `- 模型: ${meta.metadata.model_name}\n`;
250:     summary += `- 权限模式: ${meta.metadata.permission_mode}\n`;
251:     summary += `- 创建时间: ${meta.created_at}\n`;
252:     summary += `- 更新时间: ${meta.updated_at}\n\n`;

255:     for (const msg of conversation) {

268:     for (const tc of toolCalls) {

270:     }

64:           ? msg.content.substring(0, 150) + '...'

86:       }

1: import { Readable, Writable } from 'stream';
2: 
3: export interface AcpInitializeResult {
4:   protocolVersion: number;
5:   agentCapabilities: {
6:     _meta?: Record<string, any>;
7:     loadSession?: boolean;
8:     promptCapabilities?: Record<string, any>;
9:     mcpCapabilities?: { http: boolean; sse: boolean };
10:     sessionCapabilities?: Record<string, any>;
11:   };
12:   agentInfo?: { name: string; title: string; version: string };
13:   authMethods: any[];
14:   _meta?: Record<string, any>;
15: }
16: 
17: export interface AcpSessionNewResult {
18:   sessionId: string;
19:   _meta?: Record<string, any>;
20:   models?: { availableModels: any[]; currentModelId: string };
21:   modes?: { availableModes: any[]; currentModeId: string };
22: }
23: 
24: export interface AcpSessionUpdate {
25:   sessionId: string;
26:   update: {
27:     sessionUpdate: string;
28:     content?: { type: string; text: string };
29:     [key: string]: any;
30:   };
31: }
32: 
33: export interface AcpSessionPromptResult {
34:   stopReason: string;
35:   [key: string]: any;
36: }
37: 
38: type PendingRequest = {
39:   resolve: (value: any) => void;
40:   reject: (error: Error) => void;
41: };
42: 
43: export class AcpClient {
44:   private stdin: Writable;
45:   private stdout: Readable;
46:   private stderr: Readable;
47:   private messageId = 0;
48:   private pendingRequests: Map<number, PendingRequest> = new Map();
49:   private requestTimeouts: Map<number, NodeJS.Timeout> = new Map();
50:   private initialized = false;
51:   private sessionId: string | null = null;
52:   private onUpdates: Array<(update: AcpSessionUpdate) => void> = [];
53:   private buffer = '';
54:   private readonly REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 60000;
55: 
56:   constructor(stdin: Writable, stdout: Readable, stderr: Readable) {
57:     this.stdin = stdin;
58:     this.stdout = stdout;
59:     this.stderr = stderr;
60: 
61:     const stderrChunks: string[] = [];
62:     stderr.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => {
63:       stderrChunks.push(chunk.toString());
64:     });
65: 
66:     this.stdout.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => {
67:       this.buffer += chunk.toString();
68:       const lines = this.buffer.split('\n');
69:       this.buffer = lines.pop() || '';
70:       for (const line of lines) {
71:         if (line.trim()) {
72:           this.handleMessages(line);
73:         }
74:       }
75:     });
76:   }
77: 
78:   async initialize(clientInfo = { name: 'trae-plugin-cc', version: '1.0.0' }): Promise<AcpInitializeResult> {
79:     if (this.initialized) {
80:       throw new Error('Already initialized');
81:     }
82: 
83:     const result = await this.request('initialize', {
84:       protocolVersion: 1,
85:       clientCapabilities: {},
86:       clientInfo,
87:     });
88: 
89:     this.initialized = true;
90:     return result;
91:   }
92: 
93:   async createSession(cwd: string, mcpServers: any[] = []): Promise<AcpSessionNewResult> {
94:     if (!this.initialized) {
95:       throw new Error('Not initialized. Call initialize() first');
96:     }
97: 
98:     const result = await this.request('session/new', {
99:       cwd,
100:       mcpServers,
101:     });
102: 
103:     this.sessionId = result.sessionId;
104:     return result;
105:   }
106: 
107:   async loadSession(sessionId: string, cwd: string, mcpServers: any[] = []): Promise<void> {
108:     if (!this.initialized) {
109:       throw new Error('Not initialized. Call initialize() first');
110:     }
111: 
112:     await this.request('session/load', {
113:       sessionId,
114:       cwd,
115:       mcpServers,
116:     });
117: 
118:     this.sessionId = sessionId;
119:   }
120: 
121:   async sessionPrompt(
122:     prompt: string,
123:     onUpdate?: (update: AcpSessionUpdate) => void
124:   ): Promise<AcpSessionPromptResult> {
125:     if (!this.sessionId) {
126:       throw new Error('No active session. Call createSession() or loadSession() first');
127:     }
128: 
129:     if (onUpdate) {
130:       this.onUpdates.push(onUpdate);
131:     }
132: 
133:     try {
134:       return await this.request('session/prompt', {
135:         sessionId: this.sessionId,
136:         prompt: [{ type: 'text', text: prompt }],
137:       });
138:     } finally {
139:       if (onUpdate) {
140:         this.onUpdates = this.onUpdates.filter((fn) => fn !== onUpdate);
141:       }
142:     }
143:   }
144: 
145:   async sessionCancel(): Promise<void> {
146:     if (!this.sessionId) {
147:       throw new Error('No active session');
148:     }
149: 
150:     await this.request('session/cancel', { sessionId: this.sessionId });
151:   }
152: 
153:   getSessionId(): string | null {
154:     return this.sessionId;
155:   }
156: 
157:   private handleMessages(line: string) {
158:     let message: any;
159:     try {
160:       message = JSON.parse(line);
161:     } catch {
162:       return;
163:     }
164: 
165:     if (message.id !== undefined && this.pendingRequests.has(message.id)) {
166:       const pending = this.pendingRequests.get(message.id)!;
167:       const timeout = this.requestTimeouts.get(message.id);
168:       if (timeout) {
169:         clearTimeout(timeout);
170:         this.requestTimeouts.delete(message.id);
171:       }
172:       this.pendingRequests.delete(message.id);
173: 
174:       if (message.error) {
175:         pending.reject(new Error(message.error.message || JSON.stringify(message.error)));
176:       } else {
177:         pending.resolve(message.result);
178:       }
179:     } else if (message.method === 'session/update' && message.params) {
180:       for (const fn of this.onUpdates) {
181:         fn(message.params);
182:       }
183:     }
184:   }
185: 
186:   private request(method: string, params: any): Promise<any> {
187:     const id = ++this.messageId;
188: 
189:     const message = {
190:       jsonrpc: '2.0',
191:       id,
192:       method,
193:       params,
194:     };
195: 
196:     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
197:       const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
198:         this.pendingRequests.delete(id);
199:         this.requestTimeouts.delete(id);
200:         reject(new Error(`Request timeout: ${method} (${this.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS}ms)`));
201:       }, this.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS);
202: 
203:       this.pendingRequests.set(id, { resolve, reject });
204:       this.requestTimeouts.set(id, timeout);
205: 
206:       this.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(message) + '\n', (err) => {
207:         if (err) {
208:           clearTimeout(timeout);
209:           this.pendingRequests.delete(id);
210:           this.requestTimeouts.delete(id);
211:           reject(new Error(`Failed to write to stdin: ${err.message}`));
212:         }
213:       });
214:     });
215:   }
216: }

(End of file - total 216 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import { spawn, ChildProcess } from 'child_process';
2: import { AcpClient } from '../utils/acp-client';
3: import { buildSpawnEnv } from './env';
4: 
5: type StartupEvent = {
6:   at: string;
7:   event: string;
8:   detail?: string;
9: };
10: 
11: export class AcpServerManager {
12:   private process: ChildProcess | null = null;
13:   private client: AcpClient | null = null;
14: 
15:   async start(options?: { yolo?: boolean; allowedTools?: string[]; disabledTools?: string[] }): Promise<{
16:     client: AcpClient;
17:   }> {
18:     if (this.isRunning()) {
19:       return {
20:         client: this.client!,
21:       };
22:     }
23: 
24:     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
25:       const args: string[] = ['acp', 'serve'];
26: 
27:       if (options?.yolo) args.push('--yolo');
28:       if (options?.allowedTools) {
29:         for (const tool of options.allowedTools) {
30:           args.push('--allowed-tool', tool);
31:         }
32:       }
33:       if (options?.disabledTools) {
34:         for (const tool of options.disabledTools) {
35:           args.push('--disabled-tool', tool);
36:         }
37:       }
38: 
39:       const startupEvents: StartupEvent[] = [];
40:       const outputSnippets: string[] = [];
41:       let started = false;
42:       let settled = false;
43: 
44:       const recordEvent = (event: string, detail?: string) => {
45:         startupEvents.push({
46:           at: new Date().toISOString(),
47:           event,
48:           detail,
49:         });
50:       };
51: 
52:       const rememberOutput = (source: 'stdout' | 'stderr', text: string) => {
53:         const normalized = text.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
54:         if (!normalized) return;
55:         outputSnippets.push(`[${source}] ${normalized}`);
56:         if (outputSnippets.length > 8) outputSnippets.shift();
57:       };
58: 
59:       const buildDiagnostic = () => {
60:         const trace = startupEvents
61:           .map((item, idx) => `${idx + 1}. ${item.at} ${item.event}${item.detail ? ` | ${item.detail}` : ''}`)
62:           .join('\n');
63:         const recentOutput = outputSnippets.length > 0
64:           ? outputSnippets.map((line, idx) => `${idx + 1}. ${line}`).join('\n')
65:           : 'none';
66:         return `\n[ACP] startup diagnostics\ntrace:\n${trace || 'none'}\nrecent_output:\n${recentOutput}`;
67:       };
68: 
69:       const fail = (message: string) => {
70:         if (settled) return;
71:         settled = true;
72:         reject(new Error(`${message}${buildDiagnostic()}`));
73:       };
74: 
75:       const succeed = () => {
76:         if (settled) return;
77:         settled = true;
78:         const client = new AcpClient(child.stdin!, child.stdout!, child.stderr!);
79:         this.process = child;
80:         this.client = client;
81:         recordEvent('stdio:ready');
82:         resolve({ client });
83:       };
84: 
85:       const env = buildSpawnEnv();
86: 
87:       recordEvent('spawn:start', `cmd=trae-cli ${args.join(' ')}`);
88: 
89:       // 关键修复：使用 pipe 而不是 ignore, 保持 stdin 打开
90:       // ACP 协议使用 STDIO JSON-RPC, 需要 stdin 保持连接
91:       const child = spawn('trae-cli', args, {
92:         stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
93:         env,
94:       });
95: 
96:       recordEvent('spawn:created', `pid=${child.pid || 'unknown'}`);
97: 
98:       child.stdout?.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => {
99:         rememberOutput('stdout', chunk.toString());
100:       });
101: 
102:       child.stderr?.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => {
103:         rememberOutput('stderr', chunk.toString());
104:       });
105: 
106:       child.on('error', (err) => {
107:         recordEvent('process:error', err.message);
108:         fail(`ACP server spawn failed: ${err.message}`);
109:       });
110: 
111:       child.on('close', (code, signal) => {
112:         recordEvent('process:close', `code=${code ?? 'null'}, signal=${signal ?? 'null'}`);
113:         this.process = null;
114:         if (!started) {
115:           fail(`ACP server exited before ready (code=${code ?? 'null'}, signal=${signal ?? 'null'})`);
116:         }
117:       });
118: 
119:       // 等待进程启动，认证过程可能需要较长时间
120:       setTimeout(() => {
121:         if (!started && !settled) {
122:           const alive = child.exitCode === null;
123:           recordEvent('startup:timeout', `alive=${alive}, exitCode=${child.exitCode ?? 'null'}`);
124: 
125:           if (alive) {
126:             started = true;
127:             succeed();
128:             return;
129:           }
130: 
131:           fail('ACP server startup timeout (15s)');
132:         }
133:       }, 10000);
134:     });
135:   }
136: 
137:   async stop(): Promise<void> {
138:     if (this.process) {
139:       this.process.stdin?.end();
140:       this.process.kill('SIGTERM');
141:       await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
142:         setTimeout(() => {
143:           if (this.process) {
144:             this.process.kill('SIGKILL');
145:           }
146:           resolve();
147:         }, 3000);
148:       });
149:       this.process = null;
150:       this.client = null;
151:     }
152:   }
153: 
154:   isRunning(): boolean {
155:     return this.process !== null && this.process.exitCode === null;
156:   }
157: 
158:   getClient(): AcpClient | null {
159:     return this.client;
160:   }
161: 
162:   getStatus(): {
163:     running: boolean;
164:     baseUrl: string;
165:   } {
166:     return {
167:       running: this.isRunning(),
168:       baseUrl: this.isRunning() ? 'stdio://trae-cli' : '',
169:     };
170:   }
171: }

(End of file - total 171 lines)
</content>

1: import { execSync } from 'child_process';
2: 
3: export interface DiffEstimate {
4:   baseBranch: string;
5:   linesAdded: number;
6:   linesDeleted: number;
7:   filesChanged: number;
8:   untrackedFiles: string[];
9:   estimatedTime: 'quick' | 'moderate' | 'lengthy' | 'very_large';
10:   recommendation: {
11:     useBackground: boolean;
12:     reason: string;
13:   };
14: }
15: 
16: export async function detectBaseBranch(): Promise<string> {
17:   const commonBranches = ['main', 'master', 'develop', 'dev', 'mainline'];
18: 
19:   // Check current branch's upstream
20:   try {
21:     const upstream = execSync('git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic-full-name @{u}', {
22:       encoding: 'utf-8'
23:     }).trim();
24: 
25:     if (upstream) {
26:       const base = upstream.split('/').pop();
27:       if (base && commonBranches.includes(base.toLowerCase())) {
28:         return base;
29:       }
30:     }
31:   } catch {
32:     // Ignore
33:   }
34: 
35:   // Check if current branch is one of common branches
36:   try {
37:     const currentBranch = execSync('git branch --show-current', { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim();
38:     if (commonBranches.includes(currentBranch.toLowerCase())) {
39:       return 'main';
40:     }
41:   } catch {
42:     // Ignore
43:   }
44: 
45:   // Try to find default branch from remote
46:   try {
47:     const remoteDefault = execSync('git remote show origin | grep "HEAD branch" | sed "s/.*: //"', {
48:       encoding: 'utf-8'
49:     }).trim();
50: 
51:     if (remoteDefault) {
52:       return remoteDefault;
53:     }
54:   } catch {
55:     // Ignore
56:   }
57: 
58:   // Fallback to common branches
59:   for (const branch of commonBranches) {
60:     try {
61:       execSync(`git rev-parse --verify ${branch}`, { stdio: 'ignore' });
62:       return branch;
63:     } catch {
64:       continue;
65:     }
66:   }
67: 
68:   return 'main';
69: }
70: 
71: export async function getUntrackedFiles(): Promise<string[]> {
72:   try {
73:     const output = execSync('git status --porcelain', { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim();
74:     if (!output) return [];
75: 
76:     return output
77:       .split('\n')
78:       .filter(line => line.startsWith('??'))
79:       .map(line => line.substring(3).trim());
80:   } catch {
81:     return [];
82:   }
83: }
84: 
85: export async function estimateReviewSize(baseBranch: string): Promise<DiffEstimate> {
86:   let linesAdded = 0;
87:   let linesDeleted = 0;
88:   let filesChanged = 0;
89: 
90:   // Get diff stats - using fixed commands only
91:   try {
92:     const diffStats = execSync(`git diff --shortstat ${baseBranch}...HEAD`, {
93:       encoding: 'utf-8'
94:     }).trim();
95: 
96:     if (diffStats) {
97:       const match = diffStats.match(/(\d+)\s+files? changed.*?(\d+)\s+insertions.*?(\d+)\s+deletions/);
98:       if (match) {
99:         filesChanged = parseInt(match[1]) || 0;
100:         linesAdded = parseInt(match[2]) || 0;
101:         linesDeleted = parseInt(match[3]) || 0;
102:       }
103:     }
104:   } catch {
105:     // Fallback to unstaged
106:     try {
107:       const diffStats = execSync('git diff --shortstat', { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim();
108:       if (diffStats) {
109:         const match = diffStats.match(/(\d+)\s+files? changed/);
110:         if (match) {
111:           filesChanged = parseInt(match[1]) || 0;
112:         }
113:       }
114:     } catch {
115:       // Ignore
116:     }
117:   }
118: 
119:   // Get untracked files
120:   const untrackedFiles = await getUntrackedFiles();
121:   const totalChanges = linesAdded + linesDeleted;
122: 
123:   // Determine estimated time
124:   let estimatedTime: DiffEstimate['estimatedTime'];
125:   if (totalChanges < 100) {
126:     estimatedTime = 'quick';
127:   } else if (totalChanges < 500) {
128:     estimatedTime = 'moderate';
129:   } else if (totalChanges < 2000) {
130:     estimatedTime = 'lengthy';
131:   } else {
132:     estimatedTime = 'very_large';
133:   }
134: 
135:   // Determine recommendation - based on tracked changes, not untracked
136:   let useBackground = false;
137:   let reason = '';
138: 
139:   if (totalChanges < 100) {
140:     useBackground = false;
141:     reason = '变更较小，可以同步等待';
142:   } else if (totalChanges < 500) {
143:     useBackground = true;
144:     reason = '变更适中，建议后台运行';
145:   } else if (totalChanges < 2000) {
146:     useBackground = true;
147:     reason = '变更较大，建议后台运行';
148:   } else {
149:     useBackground = true;
150:     reason = '变更非常大，建议后台运行或拆分审查';
151:   }
152: 
153:   return {
154:     baseBranch,
155:     linesAdded,
156:     linesDeleted,
157:     filesChanged,
158:     untrackedFiles,
159:     estimatedTime,
160:     recommendation: { useBackground, reason }
161:   };
162: }
163: 
164: export function formatEstimate(estimate: DiffEstimate): string {
165:   const lines = [
166:     '📊 审查估算:',
167:     `   基准分支: ${estimate.baseBranch}`,
168:     `   变更: +${estimate.linesAdded} -${estimate.linesDeleted} 行`,
169:     `   文件: ${estimate.filesChanged} 个`,
170:   ];
171: 
172:   if (estimate.untrackedFiles.length > 0) {
173:     lines.push(`   未跟踪: ${estimate.untrackedFiles.length} 个文件`);
174:   }
175: 
176:   const timeMap = {
177:     quick: '快速 (1-2分钟)',
178:     moderate: '中等 (5-10分钟)',
179:     lengthy: '较长 (10-30分钟)',
180:     very_large: '非常长 (30分钟+)'
181:   };
182: 
183:   lines.push(`   预计时间: ${timeMap[estimate.estimatedTime]}`);
184:   lines.push('');
185:   lines.push(`💡 建议: ${estimate.recommendation.reason}`);
186: 
187:   return lines.join('\n');
188: }

(End of file - total 188 lines)
</content>

If you `git pull` new source changes, run `npm run build` again. 111: 112: ## Quick Start 113: 114: **Claude Code** (slash commands — user types directly): 115: 116: ``` 117: /trae:run "重构用户认证模块" # execute task 118: /trae:run "修复登录bug" --yolo # skip tool confirmations 119: /trae:review # standard review 120: /trae:adversarial-review # adversarial review 121: /trae:sessions list # list sessions 122: /trae:acp run "分析代码质量" # execute via ACP 123: ``` 124: 125: Claude Code additionally auto-registers 4 MCP tools (`trae_run`, `trae_review`, `trae_sessions`, `trae_acp`) for AI-autonomous invocation. 126: 127: **OpenCode** (Bun tools — agent calls tools): 128: 129: | Tool | CLI | Description | 130: |------|-----|-------------| 131: | `trae-setup` | `setup` | Check env & auth | 132: | `trae-run` | `run` | Execute task | 133: | `trae-review` | `review` / `adversarial-review` | Code review | 134: | `trae-sessions` | `sessions` | Session management (9 actions) | 135: | `trae-acp` | `acp` | ACP protocol (6 actions) | 136: | `trae-status` | `status` | Background task status | 137: | `trae-result` | `result` | Get task output | 138: | `trae-cancel` | `cancel` | Cancel task | 139: | `trae-rescue` | `rescue` | Failure diagnosis | 140: 141: OpenCode's TUI `/` panel lists available tools from `.opencode/tools/`; `trae-` prefix comes from filenames, not from this plugin. 142: 143: ## Commands Reference 144: 145: All commands map to `node dist/index.js [args]`. 146: In **Claude Code**, users type `/trae:` (slash commands). 147: In **OpenCode**, agents call `trae-` tools or follow command descriptions. 148: 149: ### `setup` 150: 151: Verify trae-cli installation and auth status. 152: 153:

Development 323: 324: ```bash 325: npm run build # esbuild → dist/index.js (run after ANY src/ change) 326: npm test # jest (all tests) 327: cd .opencode && npm install && cd .. # OpenCode deps (once) 328: ``` 329: 330: ### Build After Pulling Updates 331: 332: When you pull new code from the repository: 333: 334: ```bash 335: git pull 336: npm run build # required if src/ files changed 337: ``` 338: 339: > `npm install` also builds (via `postinstall`), so either command works after a `git pull`. 340: 341: ### ⚠️ Build Rule 342: 343: `npm run build` uses **esbuild** (bundled single file with shebang) — this is the **production entrypoint**. `npm run build:tsc` produces separate `.js` files for module imports but is not used at runtime. 344: 345: | Command | Output | Used By | 346: |---------|--------|---------| 347: | `npm run build` | `dist/index.js` (bundled) | **Runtime CLI** | 348: | `npm run build:tsc` | `dist/**/*.js` (separate) | Direct module imports | 349: 350: **Always run `npm run build` after modifying `src/` before testing.** This applies to OpenCode too — its tools spawn `dist/index.js`. 351: 352: ## Session Cache Paths 353: 354: trae-cli stores sessions on disk (read directly by this plugin): 355: 356: | Platform | Path | 357: |----------|------| 358: | macOS | `~/Library/Caches/trae_cli/sessions/` | 359: | Linux | `~/.cache/trae_cli/sessions/` | 360: 361: ## metadata.preview:
# trae-plugin-cc

The plugin bridges [**Claude Code**](https://claude.ai/code) and [**OpenCode**](https://opencode.ai) with ByteDance [**Trae Agent**](https://docs.trae.cn/cli) (`trae-cli`). Delegate natural language tasks, run code reviews, manage sessions, and enable Agent-to-Agent communication — all inheriting trae-cli's OAuth2 auth (no extra API keys needed).

## Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Task Delegation** | Send natural language prompts to Trae Agent with YOLO mode, session resume, git worktree isolation, and fine-grained tool permissions |
| **Code Review** | Automatic git diff + professional review — standard or adversarial (extremely strict), with smart base branch detection and change-size estimation |
| **Session Management** | Query trae-cli's history: conversations, tool calls, context summaries, topic search, and bulk cleanup |
| **ACP Protocol** | JSON-RPC over STDIO for inter-agent communication (start/stop servers, discover agents, execute/stream tasks) |
| **Background Tasks** | Long-running tasks with PID tracking, status listing, result retrieval, and cancellation |
| **Lifecycle Hooks** | Auto-triggered on session start/end/stop: env checks, background task reminders, uncommitted-change gates |

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        trae-plugin-cc                             │

1: ---
2: description: 将任务委托给 Trae Agent 执行
3: allowed-tools: Bash(trae-cli:*)
4: ---
5: 
6: # /trae:run
7: 
8: **Description:**
9: 将任务描述直接交给 Trae Agent (`trae-cli`) 执行。支持会话恢复、工具控制、结构化输出、隔离工作树等高级选项。
10: 
11: **Usage:**
12: ```bash
13: /trae:run "自然语言任务描述" [options]
14: ```
15: 
16: **Options:**
17: - `--background`: 将任务放到后台运行
18: - `--json`: 返回结构化 JSON 输出 (含 session_id)
19: - `--yolo` / `-y`: YOLO 模式，跳过工具权限确认
20: - `--resume [SESSION_ID]`: 恢复会话，不指定 ID 则自动恢复最近会话
21: - `--session-id <id>`: 指定新会话 ID
22: - `--worktree [NAME]` / `-w`: 在隔离的 git worktree 中工作
23: - `--allowed-tool <name>`: 自动批准的工具 (可多次指定)
24: - `--disallowed-tool <name>`: 自动拒绝的工具 (可多次指定)
25: - `--query-timeout <duration>`: 单次查询超时 (如 30s, 5m)
26: - `--bash-tool-timeout <duration>`: Bash 工具超时
27: - `--inject-context <session-id>`: 注入指定会话的上下文到 prompt 中
28: - `-c k=v`: 覆盖配置项 (可多次指定)
29: 
30: **Examples:**
31: ```bash
32: /trae:run "重构用户模块"
33: /trae:run "修复bug" --yolo
34: /trae:run "生成文档" --background
35: /trae:run "分析项目" --json
36: /trae:run "继续任务" --resume
37: /trae:run "继续" --resume 0d3cbdc3-e365-468e-982c-fb3d5849f5cc
38: /trae:run "实验性修改" --worktree
39: /trae:run "执行脚本" --allowed-tool Bash --allowed-tool Edit
40: /trae:run "长任务" --query-timeout 5m
41: /trae:run "继续优化" --inject-context 0d3cbdc3-e365-468e-982c-fb3d5849f5cc
42: ```
43: 
44: **Internal Execution:**
45: ```bash
46: node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/dist/index.js" run "任务描述" [options]
47: ```

(End of file - total 47 lines)
</content>

# /trae:rescue

**Description:**
当任务执行失败时，使用 Trae Agent 进行故障诊断。它会收集最近的错误日志、Git 状态等信息，并让 Trae 分析问题原因和恢复建议。

**Usage:**
```bash
/trae:rescue [--context "额外信息"] [--retries 3] [--force]
```

**Options:**
- `--context <text>`: 提供额外的上下文信息帮助诊断
- `--retries <n>`: 最大重试次数 (默认: 3)
- `--force`: 跳过确认直接执行恢复操作

1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: /**
3:  * session-lifecycle-hook.mjs
4:  * Handles SessionStart and SessionEnd events for trae-plugin-cc
5:  */
6: 
7: import { execSync } from 'child_process';
8: import { existsSync, readdirSync, statSync, unlinkSync, mkdirSync, appendFileSync } from 'fs';
9: import { join } from 'path';
10: import { homedir } from 'os';
11: import { getPluginDir, getRunningJobs } from './job-utils.mjs';
12: 
13: const HOME_BIN = join(homedir(), '.local', 'bin');
14: const CONFIG_FILE = join(homedir(), '.trae', 'trae_cli.yaml');
15: 
16: // Ensure ~/.local/bin is in PATH for trae-cli detection
17: (function ensurePath() {
18:   const existing = process.env.PATH || '';
19:   if (!existing.split(':').includes(HOME_BIN)) {
20:     process.env.PATH = `${HOME_BIN}:${existing}`;
21:   }
22: })();
23: 
24: function checkTraeCliInstalled() {
25:   // Use execSync only with fixed commands, no user input
26:   try {
27:     execSync('which trae-cli', { stdio: 'ignore' });
28:     return true;
29:   } catch {
30:     return false;
31:   }
32: }
33: 
34: function checkTraeConfig() {
35:   return existsSync(CONFIG_FILE);
36: }
37: 
38: function cleanupStaleLogs() {
39:   const pluginDir = getPluginDir();
40:   if (!existsSync(pluginDir)) return;
41: 
42:   const now = Date.now();
43:   const sevenDays = 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
44: 
45:   try {
46:     const files = readdirSync(pluginDir);
47:     const logs = files.filter(f => f.endsWith('.log'));
48: 
49:     for (const logFile of logs) {
50:       const logPath = join(pluginDir, logFile);
51:       const mtime = statSync(logPath).mtimeMs;
52:       if (now - mtime > sevenDays) {
53:         try {
54:           unlinkSync(logPath);
55:           const pidFile = logFile.replace('.log', '.pid');
56:           const pidPath = join(pluginDir, pidFile);
57:           if (existsSync(pidPath)) unlinkSync(pidPath);
58:         } catch {
59:           // Ignore cleanup errors
60:         }
61:       }
62:     }
63:   } catch {
64:     // Ignore errors
65:   }
66: }
67: 
68: async function sessionStart() {
69:   console.log('🔍 [Trae Plugin] Session Start Check');
70:   console.log('─'.repeat(40));
71: 
72:   const installed = checkTraeCliInstalled();
73:   const hasConfig = checkTraeConfig();
74: 
75:   if (installed && hasConfig) {
76:     console.log('✅ trae-cli 已安装并配置');
77:   } else {
78:     if (!installed) {
79:       console.log('⚠️  trae-cli 未安装');
80:     }
81:     if (!hasConfig) {
82:       console.log('⚠️  配置文件不存在');
83:     }
84:     console.log('\n运行 /trae:setup 进行初始化');
85:   }
86: 
87:   const running = getRunningJobs();
88:   if (running.length > 0) {
89:     console.log(`📋 后台任务: ${running.length} 个运行中`);
90:   }
91: 
92:   console.log('─'.repeat(40));
93: }
94: 
95: async function sessionEnd() {
96:   console.log('🔍 [Trae Plugin] Session End Cleanup');
97:   console.log('─'.repeat(40));
98: 
99:   cleanupStaleLogs();
100: 
101:   const running = getRunningJobs();
102:   if (running.length > 0) {
103:     console.log(`⚠️  有 ${running.length} 个后台任务仍在运行:`);
104:     for (const id of running) {
105:       console.log(`   - ${id}`);
106:     }
107:     console.log('使用 /trae:status 查看状态');
108:   } else {
109:     console.log('✅ 无运行中的任务');
110:   }
111: 
112:   console.log('─'.repeat(40));
113: }
114: 
115: async function postReview() {
116:   // Post-review hook: record review execution for tracking
117:   try {
118:     const pluginDir = getPluginDir();
119:     if (!existsSync(pluginDir)) {
120:       mkdirSync(pluginDir, { recursive: true });
121:     }
122: 
123:     const reviewLog = join(pluginDir, 'reviews.jsonl');
124:     const entry = JSON.stringify({
125:       timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
126:       event: 'PostReview',
127:       cwd: process.cwd(),
128:     }) + '\n';
129: 
130:     appendFileSync(reviewLog, entry);
131:     console.log('📝 审查记录已保存:', reviewLog);
132:   } catch (e) {
133:     console.error('⚠️  审查记录保存失败:', e.message);
134:   }
135:   process.exit(0);
136: }
137: 
138: // Main handler
139: const hookType = process.argv[2];
140: 
141: switch (hookType) {
142:   case 'SessionStart':
143:     sessionStart();
144:     break;
145:   case 'SessionEnd':
146:     sessionEnd();
147:     break;
148:   case 'PostReview':
149:     postReview();
150:     break;
151:   default:
152:     console.error('Unknown hook type:', hookType);
153:     process.exit(1);
154: }

(End of file - total 154 lines)
</content>

1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: /**
3:  * stop-review-gate-hook.mjs
4:  * Optional review gate that prompts for review before stopping
5:  */
6: 
7: import { execSync } from 'child_process';
8: import { getRunningJobs } from './job-utils.mjs';
9: 
10: function hasUncommittedChanges() {
11:   try {
12:     // Check for staged, unstaged, and untracked changes
13:     const staged = execSync('git diff --cached --name-only', { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim();
14:     if (staged) return true;
15: 
16:     const unstaged = execSync('git diff --name-only', { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim();
17:     if (unstaged) return true;
18: 
19:     const untracked = execSync('git status --porcelain', { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim();
20:     if (untracked) return true;
21: 
22:     return false;
23:   } catch {
24:     return false;
25:   }
26: }
27: 
28: async function stopGate() {
29:   const force = process.argv.includes('--force');
30: 
31:   if (force) {
32:     console.log('⏭️  [Trae Plugin] Stop gate bypassed (--force)');
33:     process.exit(0);
34:   }
35: 
36:   const hasChanges = hasUncommittedChanges();
37:   const runningJobs = getRunningJobs();
38: 
39:   if (!hasChanges && runningJobs.length === 0) {
40:     process.exit(0);
41:   }
42: 
43:   console.log('⚠️  [Trae Plugin] Stop Gate');
44:   console.log('─'.repeat(40));
45: 
46:   if (hasChanges) {
47:     console.log('📝 检测到未提交的代码变更');

50: 
51:   if (runningJobs.length > 0) {
52:     console.log(`📋 有 ${runningJobs.length} 个后台任务仍在运行`);
53:   }
54: 
55:   console.log('─'.repeat(40));
56:   console.log('使用 --force 参数可强制退出');
57: 
58:   process.exit(1);
59: }
60: 
61: stopGate();

1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: /**
3:  * job-utils.mjs - Shared utilities for background job management
4:  */
5: 
6: import { existsSync, readdirSync, readFileSync } from 'fs';
7: import { join } from 'path';
8: 
9: const PLUGIN_DIR = '.claude-trae-plugin';
10: 
11: export function getPluginDir(baseDir = process.cwd()) {
12:   return join(baseDir, PLUGIN_DIR);
13: }
14: 
15: export function getRunningJobs() {
16:   const pluginDir = getPluginDir();
17:   if (!existsSync(pluginDir)) return [];
18: 
19:   try {
20:     const files = readdirSync(pluginDir);
21:     const pidFiles = files.filter(f => f.endsWith('.pid'));
22:     const running = [];
23: 
24:     for (const pidFile of pidFiles) {
25:       try {
26:         const pid = parseInt(readFileSync(join(pluginDir, pidFile), 'utf-8').trim());
27:         process.kill(pid, 0);
28:         running.push(pidFile.replace('.pid', ''));
29:       } catch {
30:       }
31:     }
32:     return running;
33:   } catch {
34:     return [];
35:   }
36: }
37: 
38: export function isJobRunning(jobName) {
39:   const runningJobs = getRunningJobs();
40:   return runningJobs.includes(jobName);
41: }

(End of file - total 41 lines)
</content>

3:  * trae-companion.mjs - Unified entry point for trae-plugin-cc
4:  */
5: 
6: import { existsSync } from 'fs';
7: import { join, dirname } from 'path';
8: import { fileURLToPath } from 'url';
9: import { execSync, spawn } from 'child_process';
10: import { getRunningJobs } from './job-utils.mjs';
11: 
12: const __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);
13: const __dirname = dirname(__filename);
14: const PLUGIN_ROOT = join(__dirname, '..');
15: 
16: const SUPPORTED_COMMANDS = [
17:   'setup', 'review', 'adversarial-review', 'run',
18:   'status', 'result', 'cancel', 'rescue', 'estimate'
19: ];
20: 
21: const RAW_ARGS = process.argv.slice(2);
22: const FLAGS = {
23:   json: RAW_ARGS.includes('--json'),
24:   verbose: RAW_ARGS.includes('--verbose')
25: };
26: 
27: function outputJson(data) {
28:   if (FLAGS.json) console.log(JSON.stringify(data, null, 2));
29: }
30: 
31: function error(msg) {
32:   console.error(msg);
33:   if (FLAGS.json) console.log(JSON.stringify({ error: msg }, null, 2));
34: }
35: 
36: function checkTraeCli() {
37:   try {
38:     execSync('which trae-cli', { stdio: 'ignore' });
39:     return true;
40:   } catch { return false; }
41: }
42: 
43: function checkTraeConfig() {
44:   return existsSync(join(process.cwd(), '.trae', 'trae_config.yaml'));
45: }
46: 
47: function detectBaseBranch() {
48:   const branches = ['main', 'master', 'develop', 'dev'];
49:   try {
50:     const upstream = execSync('git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic-full-name @{u}', { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim();
51:     if (upstream) {
52:       const base = upstream.split('/').pop();
53:       if (base && branches.includes(base.toLowerCase())) return base;
54:     }
55:   } catch {}
56:   return 'main';
57: }
58: 
59: function estimateReviewSize(baseBranch = 'main') {
60:   let added = 0, deleted = 0, files = 0, untracked = [];
61: 
62:   try {
63:     const stats = execSync(`git diff --shortstat ${baseBranch}...HEAD`, { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim();
64:     const m = stats.match(/(\d+)\s+files? changed.*?(\d+)\s+insertions.*?(\d+)\s+deletions/);
65:     if (m) { files = parseInt(m[1]) || 0; added = parseInt(m[2]) || 0; deleted = parseInt(m[3]) || 0; }
66:   } catch {}
67: 
68:   try {
69:     const status = execSync('git status --porcelain', { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim();
70:     if (status) untracked = status.split('\n').filter(l => l.startsWith('??')).map(l => l.substring(3).trim());
71:   } catch {}
72: 
73:   const total = added + deleted;
74:   let time = 'quick', bg = false, reason = '变更较小，可以同步等待';
75: 
76:   if (total >= 100 && total < 500) { time = 'moderate'; bg = true; reason = '变更适中，建议后台运行'; }
77:   else if (total >= 500 && total < 2000) { time = 'lengthy'; bg = true; reason = '变更较大，建议后台运行'; }
78:   else if (total >= 2000) { time = 'very_large'; bg = true; reason = '变更非常大，建议后台运行或拆分'; }
79: 
80:   return { baseBranch, linesAdded: added, linesDeleted: deleted, filesChanged: files, untrackedFiles: untracked, estimatedTime: time, recommendation: { useBackground: bg, reason } };
81: }
82: 
83: async function runSetup() {
84:   const installed = checkTraeCli();
85:   const configured = checkTraeConfig();
86:   if (FLAGS.json) {
87:     outputJson({ command: 'setup', installed, configured, status: installed && configured ? 'ready' : 'needs_setup' });
88:   } else {
89:     console.log('🔍 [Trae] Setup Check');
90:     console.log(installed && configured ? '✅ OK' : '⚠️ 需要配置');
91:   }
92: }
93: 
94: async function runEstimate(args) {
95:   let base = args.includes('--base') ? args[args.indexOf('--base') + 1] : detectBaseBranch();
96:   const est = estimateReviewSize(base);
97:   if (FLAGS.json) outputJson({ command: 'estimate', ...est });
98:   else {
99:     console.log(`📊 基准分支: ${est.baseBranch}, 变更: +${est.linesAdded}/-${est.linesDeleted}, 文件: ${est.filesChanged}`);
100:     console.log(`💡 ${est.recommendation.reason}`);
101:   }
102: }
103: 
104: async function runStatus() {
105:   const jobs = getRunningJobs();
106:   if (FLAGS.json) outputJson({ command: 'status', running: jobs.length, jobs });
107:   else console.log(jobs.length ? `运行中: ${jobs.length}个` : '无运行任务');
108: }
109: 
110: async function main() {
111:   if (!RAW_ARGS.length) { console.log('用法: trae-companion <cmd>'); process.exit(1); }
112: 
113:   const cmd = RAW_ARGS[0];
114:   if (!SUPPORTED_COMMANDS.includes(cmd)) { error(`未知命令: ${cmd}`); process.exit(1); }
115: 
116:   const cmdArgs = RAW_ARGS.slice(1).filter(a => a !== '--json' && a !== '--verbose');
117: 
118:   try {
119:     switch (cmd) {
120:       case 'setup': await runSetup(); break;
121:       case 'estimate': await runEstimate(cmdArgs); break;
122:       case 'status': await runStatus(); break;
123:       default:
124:         const cli = join(PLUGIN_ROOT, 'dist', 'index.js');
125:         const child = spawn('node', [cli, cmd, ...cmdArgs], { stdio: 'inherit' });
126:         child.on('close', process.exit);
127:     }
128:   } catch (e) { error(e.message); process.exit(1); }
129: }
130: 
131: main();

(End of file - total 131 lines)
</content>

1: import type { Plugin } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"
2: import path from "path"
3: 
4: const SCRIPTS_DIR = path.join(path.dirname(path.dirname(import.meta.dir)), "scripts")
5: 
6: function hookArgs(script: string, hookType: string): string[] {
7:   return ["node", path.join(SCRIPTS_DIR, script), hookType]
8: }
9: 
10: async function callHook(
11:   script: string,
12:   hookType: string,
13:   cwd: string,
14:   payload?: Record<string, unknown>,
15: ) {
16:   try {
17:     const proc = Bun.spawn(hookArgs(script, hookType), {
18:       cwd,
19:       stdout: "pipe",
20:       stderr: "pipe",
21:       stdin: payload ? new Blob([JSON.stringify(payload) + "\n"]) : undefined,
22:     })
23:     const stdout = await new Response(proc.stdout).text()
24:     const stderr = await new Response(proc.stderr).text()
25:     await proc.exited
26:     if (stdout) console.log(`[trae:${hookType}]`, stdout.trim())
27:     if (stderr) console.warn(`[trae:${hookType}]`, stderr.trim())
28:   } catch (e) {
29:     console.error(`[trae-hook:${hookType}]`, e)
30:   }
31: }
32: 
33: function callHookSync(
34:   script: string,
35:   hookType: string,
36:   cwd: string,
37:   payload?: Record<string, unknown>,
38: ) {
39:   try {
40:     const result = Bun.spawnSync(hookArgs(script, hookType), {
41:       cwd,
42:       stdout: "pipe",
43:       stderr: "pipe",
44:       stdin: payload ? new TextEncoder().encode(JSON.stringify(payload) + "\n") : undefined,
45:     })
46:     const stdout = new TextDecoder().decode(result.stdout)
47:     const stderr = new TextDecoder().decode(result.stderr)
48:     if (stdout) console.log(`[trae:${hookType}]`, stdout.trim())
49:     if (stderr) console.warn(`[trae:${hookType}]`, stderr.trim())
50:   } catch (e) {
51:     console.error(`[trae-hook-sync:${hookType}]`, e)
52:   }
53: }
54: 
55: /**
56:  * Trae Plugin hooks for OpenCode
57:  *
58:  * Maps Claude Code hooks to OpenCode events:
59:  * - SessionStart  → session.created
60:  * - Stop gate     → tool.execute.after (check uncommitted changes)
61:  * - PostReview    → tool.execute.after (review tools only)
62:  * - SessionEnd    → server.instance.disposed / session.deleted
63:  */
64: export const TraeHooksPlugin: Plugin = async ({ directory }) => {
65:   let currentSessionID: string | null = null
66: 
67:   return {
68:     event: async ({ event }) => {
69:       try {
70:         switch (event.type) {
71:           case "session.created": {
72:             const session = (event as any).properties?.info
73:             if (session?.id) {
74:               currentSessionID = session.id
75:               await callHook("session-lifecycle-hook.mjs", "SessionStart", directory, {
76:                 session_id: session.id,
77:               })
78:             }
79:             break
80:           }
81: 
82:           case "session.deleted": {
83:             const session = (event as any).properties?.info
84:             if (session?.id) {
85:               callHookSync("session-lifecycle-hook.mjs", "SessionEnd", directory, {
86:                 session_id: session.id,
87:               })
88:               currentSessionID = null
89:             }
90:             break
91:           }
92: 
93:           case "server.instance.disposed": {
94:             if (currentSessionID) {
95:               callHookSync("session-lifecycle-hook.mjs", "SessionEnd", directory, {
96:                 session_id: currentSessionID,
97:               })
98:               currentSessionID = null
99:             }
100:             break
101:           }
102:         }
103:       } catch {
104:         // Silently ignore — plugin failures must not crash OpenCode
105:       }
106:     },
107:   }
108: }

(End of file - total 108 lines)

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: // Entire CLI plugin for OpenCode
2: // Auto-generated by `entire enable --agent opencode`
3: // Do not edit manually — changes will be overwritten on next install.
4: // Requires Bun runtime (used by OpenCode's plugin system for loading ESM plugins).
5: import type { Plugin } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"
6: 
7: export const EntirePlugin: Plugin = async ({ directory }) => {
8:   const ENTIRE_CMD = 'entire'
9:   // Track seen user messages to fire turn-start only once per message
10:   const seenUserMessages = new Set<string>()
11:   // Track current session ID for message events (which don't include sessionID)
12:   let currentSessionID: string | null = null
13:   // Track the model used by the most recent assistant message
14:   let currentModel: string | null = null
15:   // In-memory store for message metadata (role, tokens, etc.)
16:   const messageStore = new Map<string, any>()
17: 
18:   /**
19:    * Build the shell command for a hook invocation.
20:    * Uses sh -c so that shell command substitution in ENTIRE_CMD
21:    * (e.g., $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) for local-dev) is interpreted.
22:    */
23:   function hookCmd(hookName: string): string[] {
24:     return ["sh", "-c", `${ENTIRE_CMD} hooks opencode ${hookName}`]
25:   }
26: 
27:   /**
28:    * Pipe JSON payload to an entire hooks command (async).
29:    * Errors are logged but never thrown — plugin failures must not crash OpenCode.
30:    */
31:   async function callHook(hookName: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>) {
32:     try {
33:       const json = JSON.stringify(payload)
34:       const proc = Bun.spawn(hookCmd(hookName), {
35:         cwd: directory,
36:         stdin: new Blob([json + "\n"]),
37:         stdout: "ignore",
38:         stderr: "ignore",
39:       })
40:       await proc.exited
41:     } catch {
42:       // Silently ignore — plugin failures must not crash OpenCode
43:     }
44:   }
45: 
46:   /**
47:    * Synchronous variant for hooks that fire near process exit (turn-end, session-end).
48:    * `opencode run` breaks its event loop on the same session.status idle event that
49:    * triggers turn-end. The async callHook would be killed before completing.
50:    * Bun.spawnSync blocks the event loop, preventing exit until the hook finishes.
51:    */
52:   function callHookSync(hookName: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>) {
53:     try {
54:       const json = JSON.stringify(payload)
55:       Bun.spawnSync(hookCmd(hookName), {
56:         cwd: directory,
57:         stdin: new TextEncoder().encode(json + "\n"),
58:         stdout: "ignore",
59:         stderr: "ignore",
60:       })
61:     } catch {
62:       // Silently ignore — plugin failures must not crash OpenCode
63:     }
64:   }
65: 
66:   return {
67:     event: async ({ event }) => {
68:       try {
69:         switch (event.type) {
70:           case "session.created": {
71:             const session = (event as any).properties?.info
72:             if (!session?.id) break
73:             // Reset per-session tracking state when switching sessions.
74:             if (currentSessionID !== session.id) {
75:               seenUserMessages.clear()
76:               messageStore.clear()
77:               currentModel = null
78:               await callHook("session-start", {
79:                 session_id: session.id,
80:               })
81:             }
82:             currentSessionID = session.id
83:             break
84:           }
85: 
86:           case "message.updated": {
87:             const msg = (event as any).properties?.info
88:             if (!msg) break
89:             // Store message metadata (role, time, tokens, etc.)
90:             messageStore.set(msg.id, msg)
91:             // Track model from assistant messages
92:             if (msg.role === "assistant" && msg.modelID) {
93:               currentModel = msg.modelID
94:             }
95:             break
96:           }
97: 
98:           case "message.part.updated": {
99:             const part = (event as any).properties?.part
100:             if (!part?.messageID) break
101: 
102:             // Fire turn-start on the first text part of a new user message
103:             const msg = messageStore.get(part.messageID)
104:             if (msg?.role === "user" && part.type === "text" && !seenUserMessages.has(msg.id)) {
105:               seenUserMessages.add(msg.id)
106:               const sessionID = msg.sessionID ?? currentSessionID
107:               if (sessionID) {
108:                 await callHook("turn-start", {
109:                   session_id: sessionID,
110:                   prompt: part.text ?? "",
111:                   model: currentModel ?? "",
112:                 })
113:               }
114:             }
115:             break
116:           }
117: 
118:           case "session.status": {
119:             // session.status fires in both TUI and non-interactive (run) mode.
120:             // session.idle is deprecated and not reliably emitted in run mode.
121:             const props = (event as any).properties
122:             if (props?.status?.type !== "idle") break
123:             const sessionID = props?.sessionID ?? currentSessionID
124:             if (!sessionID) break
125:             // Use sync variant: `opencode run` exits on the same idle event,
126:             // so an async hook would be killed before completing.
127:             callHookSync("turn-end", {
128:               session_id: sessionID,
129:               model: currentModel ?? "",
130:             })
131:             break
132:           }
133: 
134:           case "session.compacted": {
135:             const sessionID = (event as any).properties?.sessionID
136:             if (!sessionID) break
137:             await callHook("compaction", {
138:               session_id: sessionID,
139:             })
140:             break
141:           }
142: 
143:           case "session.deleted": {
144:             const session = (event as any).properties?.info
145:             if (!session?.id) break
146:             seenUserMessages.clear()
147:             messageStore.clear()
148:             currentSessionID = null
149:             // Use sync variant: session-end may fire during shutdown.
150:             callHookSync("session-end", {
151:               session_id: session.id,
152:             })
153:             break
154:           }
155: 
156:           case "server.instance.disposed": {
157:             // Fires when OpenCode shuts down (TUI close or `opencode run` exit).
158:             // session.deleted only fires on explicit user deletion, not on quit,
159:             // so this is the only reliable way to end sessions on exit.
160:             if (!currentSessionID) break
161:             const sessionID = currentSessionID
162:             seenUserMessages.clear()
163:             messageStore.clear()
164:             currentSessionID = null
165:             // Use sync variant: this is the last event before process exit.
166:             callHookSync("session-end", {
167:               session_id: sessionID,
168:             })
169:             break
170:           }
171:         }
172:       } catch {
173:         // Silently ignore — plugin failures must not crash OpenCode
174:       }
175:     },
176:   }
177: }

(End of file - total 177 lines)
</content>

1: ---
2: description: Performs a standard professional code review on current Git changes. Automatically fetches git diff and submits to Trae Agent for review. Supports base branch override, background execution, and structured JSON output.
3: ---
4: 
5: Execute a standard code review by running:
6: 
7: ```bash
8: node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/dist/index.js" review $ARGUMENTS
9: ```
10: 
11: If no arguments are provided, it auto-detects the base branch and reviews all current changes.
12: 
13: Options:
14: - `--base <branch>`: Specify the base branch to compare against (default: auto-detect)
15: - `--background`: Run review in background for large changes
16: - `--yolo` / `-y`: Skip tool permission confirmations
17: - `--json`: Return structured JSON output
18: - `--session-id <id>`: Specify session ID
19: 
20: Example: `review`, `review --base develop`, `review --background`, `review --json`

<content>
1: ---
2: description: Lists or queries Trae Agent session history. View session details, conversation history, tool call records, and full context summaries. Data sourced from trae-cli session files on disk.
3: ---
4: 
5: Manage Trae Agent sessions by running:
6: 
7: ```bash
8: node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/dist/index.js" sessions $ARGUMENTS
9: ```
10: 
11: Actions:
12: - `list`: List all sessions (default)
13: - `recent`: View the most recent session
14: - `detail <id>`: View session details
15: - `conversation <id>`: Get conversation history
16: - `tools <id>`: Get tool call records
17: - `context <id>`: Get full context summary
18: - `find <topic>`: Search sessions by topic
19: - `delete <id>`: Delete a session
20: 
21: Options:
22: - `--cwd <path>`: Filter by working directory
23: - `--limit <n>`: Limit number of results (default 20)
24:

(End of file - total 25 lines)
</content>
